Data

DBPR-graded · 5,856 restaurants · Updated June 2026

Central Florida Restaurants, Graded A–F by Health Inspection

Compare Central Florida restaurants by the one thing Florida won’t tell you: how clean they are. We turn the state’s public DBPR inspection records into clear A–F health grades for 2066 restaurants — 1318 currently grade A — alongside dietary options and diner ratings. Browse by county, city, or one of the region’s named dining districts.

C Fair 70–79 180
D Poor 60–69 80

Frequently asked questions about Florida restaurant grades

Does Florida give restaurants letter grades or scores?

No. Florida's Department of Business and Professional Regulation (DBPR) inspects restaurants and records violations, but it does not assign letter grades or scores. The A–F grades on this site are calculated by us from DBPR's public inspection records.

How are the health grades for Florida restaurants calculated?

Each restaurant starts at 100 points; we subtract 8 points per high-priority violation, 3 per intermediate, and 1 per basic from its most recent inspection, plus penalties for enforcement actions. Scores map to A (90+), B (80–89), C (70–79), D (60–69), and F (below 60).

Which Florida restaurants does this guide cover?

We cover the eight Central Florida counties in DBPR District 4 — Orange, Brevard, Volusia, Seminole, Osceola, Lake, St. Lucie, and Indian River — currently 2066 graded restaurants, with more added every week.

How current is the inspection data?

We refresh from DBPR on a regular cycle, and every restaurant page shows the date its data was last updated.

Read the full method on our methodology page.

Central Florida Restaurants

Restaurant health inspection grades, dietary options, and diner ratings across Central Florida. Inspection data from Florida DBPR public records.
